extraction.one_line_summary: PETERS Nans won from a 13-rider breakaway given 7+ minutes, while BARDET Roman attacked the final climb to claw back 13 seconds lost to a crash.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway of 13 riders released within first 2 miles gained 7:25, suggesting peloton pre-negotiated GC teams' willingness to concede stage.
BARDET Roman attacked final climb immediately after teammate PETERS won, converting emotional momentum into 13-second GC recovery from earlier crash.
BENNETT Sam contested intermediate sprint despite 170km remaining and mountains ahead, revealing obsessive point-defense against SAGAN in green jersey battle.
POWLESS Neilson placed 5th from breakaway while best-placed GC rider there, prioritizing time recovery over stage win after previous day's mechanical.

rider_signals.BARDET Roman: positive: recovered crash-lost seconds via final climb attack, showing resilience but also exposing AG2R's need to gamble for time
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: contested breakaway finish despite GC focus, recovered 7 minutes day after mechanical loss showing strong climbing form
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: defended yellow by following BARDET's attack but couldn't prevent 13-second leak, suggests tactical conservatism
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: finished with YATES group to reclaim time lost previous day, showing consistency at age 20
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoit: positive: 40 KOM points available yet he prioritized polka dot consolidation from breakaway position
